title: 10 Herbal Teas to Calm Itchy Eyes and Breathe Easier
intro_desc: Herbal teas for itchy eyes are natural remedies that soothe and calm irritated eyes, providing relief from discomfort and inflammation. These teas are made from herbs that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce redness and itching. Some of the best herbal teas for itchy eyes include Calendula officinalis, Euphrasia officinalis, Symphytum officinale, Echinacea purpurea, Achillea millefolium, Aloe vera, Aloe barbadensis, Chamomilla recutita, Hypericum perforatum, and Plantago lanceolata. If left untreated, itchy eyes can lead to vision problems, increased sensitivity to light, and even permanent damage, significantly impacting daily life and quality of time spent with loved ones.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tea is a great remedy for itchy eyes because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties. The plant contains compounds called flavonoids and triterpenoids that help reduce swelling and calm irritation. When you drink Calendula officinalis tea, these compounds get into your bloodstream and travel to your eyes, soothing the itchy and red skin. This tea also has antihistamine properties, which can help block the release of histamine - a chemical that causes allergic reactions and makes your eyes itch. By reducing inflammation and histamine levels, Calendula officinalis tea can provide quick relief from itchy, irritated eyes.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin in Achillea Millefolium is good for itchy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and calm irritation in the eyes.
Saponins are good for itchy eyes because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and discomfort.
Flavonoids are good for itchy eyes because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and soothe the irritation, thereby providing relief from itchy and watery eyes.
Polyphenols are good for itchy eyes because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and calm irritated tissues, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort.
Kaempferol is good for itchy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe the eyes, calming the itch and discomfort.

remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal teas for itchy eyes
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal tea for itchy eyes, start by gathering fresh or dried leaves of this plant, also known as yarrow. If using fresh leaves, choose those that are free from dirt and any signs of wilting. Rinse the leaves with clean water and chop them into smaller pieces to release their oils. If using dried leaves, simply measure out the required amount. In a saucepan, combine 1 teaspoon of the yarrow leaves with 1 cup of boiling water. Reduce the heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the leaves to infuse into the water. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine mesh sieve or cheesecloth, and discard the leaves. Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature before drinking it. As you sip the tea, the anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties of Achillea millefolium may help to soothe itchy eyes and reduce swelling.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis tea is a popular remedy for itchy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties. The main ingredient, euphrasia, contains compounds that help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes. When you drink the tea, the chemicals pass through your body and into your bloodstream, which then carries them to your eyes. Here, they work to calm down the itchy and irritated tissues, providing quick relief. Euphrasia officinalis tea is also known to be an antioxidant, which means it helps protect your eyes from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and protecting your eyes, Euphrasia officinalis tea can provide long-term relief from itchy eyes and promote overall eye health. Additionally, the tea is often used to treat conjunctivitis, a common cause of itchy eyes, and has been shown to be effective in soothing the symptoms of this condition.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin in ACHILLEA MILLEFOLIUM is good for itchy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and calm the eyes, providing relief from itchy and irritated eyes.
Tannins are good for itchy eyes because they have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that help reduce swelling and itching in the eyes.
Kaempferol is good for itchy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe irritation, providing relief from itchy eyes.
Polyphenols are good for itchy eyes because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and soothe irritation in the eyes.
Rutin is good for itchy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce swelling and soothe the eye tissue, providing quick relief from irritation and discomfort.

remedies.image_alt: Euphrasia officinalis herbal teas for itchy eyes
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal tea to help with itchy eyes, start by gathering fresh or dried leaves and flowers of the plant, also known as Yarrow. If using fresh leaves, rinse them gently in cold water and pat them dry. If using dried leaves, simply measure out the desired amount. Combine 1 teaspoon of the leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot or a cup. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another cup. Discard the solids and let the tea cool to room temperature. You can drink the tea as is, or add a bit of honey to taste. To use as an eye wash, let the cooled tea cool to lukewarm temperature and then gently pour it over your closed eyes, allowing it to flow over the lids and the lower eyelids, then rinse with clean water. Repeat as needed, ideally 2-3 times a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea is a natural remedy that may help with itchy eyes. The plant contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and redness in the eyes. This can be especially helpful for people who experience itchy eyes due to allergies or irritants. Echinacea also has antioxidant properties, which can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. Furthermore, some studies suggest that Echinacea may have a soothing effect on the eyes, reducing the sensation of itchiness and discomfort. Drinking Echinacea purpurea tea may also help to calm the body's allergic response, which can contribute to itchy eyes. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in the tea may also have a protective effect on the eyes, helping to prevent inflammation and damage.

Quercetin is good for itchy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and calm irritation in the eyes, making it a natural remedy for soothing itchy and irritated eyes.

remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for itchy eyes
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal tea that can help with itchy eyes, start by gathering a few handfuls of dried yarrow flowers, also known as Achillea millefolium. You can usually find yarrow flowers at a local herbal store or online. Next, combine one teaspoon of the dried flowers with one cup of boiling water in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup. Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flowers to release their medicinal properties into the water. Strain the tea into a separate container using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the flower particles. You can then store the tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours. To use the tea, simply dip a cotton ball or pad into the chilled tea and apply it to your itchy eyes, or drink the tea as is to reap its benefits from the inside out.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale tea, also known as comfrey tea, is a natural remedy for itchy eyes. The tea is made from the leaves of the comfrey plant, which has been used for centuries to treat various health issues. The leaves of the comfrey plant contain a compound called allantoin, which is known for its anti-inflammatory properties. When you drink comfrey tea, the allantoin is absorbed into your body and helps to reduce swelling and irritation in your eyes. This can provide quick relief from itchy eyes caused by allergies, dryness, or other irritants. The tea also has antiseptic properties, which can help to prevent infections and promote healing in the affected area. As a result, comfrey tea can be a soothing and natural remedy for itchy eyes.

remedies.image_alt: Symphytum officinale herbal teas for itchy eyes
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal tea that helps with itchy eyes, start by gathering some fresh or dried yarrow flowers. If using fresh flowers, gently rinse them with clean water, and then dry them completely. If using dried flowers, skip this step. Next, add one teaspoon of yarrow flowers to a cup of boiling water, and let it steep for five to seven minutes. You can also add a few drops of honey or lemon to taste, but this is optional. Strain the tea into a separate container using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve, and discard the solids. Allow the tea to cool, then use it as needed to help soothe itchy eyes. You can apply the cooled tea as a compress by dipping a clean cloth into the tea and placing it over your closed eyes for a few minutes. Repeat this process whenever you need relief from itchy eyes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tea is good for itchy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes. The tea contains a compound called aloin, which is a natural soothing agent that calms the eyes. When you drink aloe barbadensis tea, the aloin is absorbed into your body and helps to reduce inflammation in the eyes. This can provide quick relief from itchy and irritated eyes. Aloe barbadensis tea also has antioxidants that help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. These free radicals can contribute to eye problems like itchy eyes, dry eyes, and even eye infections. By drinking aloe barbadensis tea, you can help keep your eyes healthy and protected. Aloe barbadensis tea can be a natural and effective remedy for itchy eyes, especially when used in combination with other treatments like eye drops or cold compresses.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphorbia peplus tea is a natural remedy that may help soothe itchy eyes. The plant contains a compound called phytolaccatoxin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, it can reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from itchy sensations. The tea is also rich in antioxidants, which help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, Euphorbia peplus tea may help to calm the nervous system, which can contribute to itchiness in the eyes. Some people find that drinking the tea helps to reduce puffiness and itchiness, allowing them to feel more comfortable. If using fresh flowers, simply pluck the delicate white or yellow blooms from the stem. If using dried flowers, you can purchase them at a local health food store or online. Next, place 1-2 teaspoons of the Yarrow flowers into a heat-resistant cup or teapot. Pour in 1 cup of boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the flowers. Discard the solids and let the tea cool to a comfortable temperature. Once cooled, you can drink the tea as needed to help alleviate itchy eyes. You can also add a bit of honey to sweeten the tea if desired. It's recommended to drink 1-2 cups of Yarrow tea per day for best results.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomilla recutita tea is a natural remedy for itchy eyes. This tea comes from the dried flowers of the chamomile plant, which are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. When we drink the tea, these compounds get into our body and help to calm down the inflammation in our eyes. The tea also has a soothing effect on the skin and tissues around our eyes, which can become itchy and irritated due to allergies or dryness. By reducing inflammation and soothing the skin, the tea can provide quick relief from itchy eyes. Additionally, the tea may help to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, making it a popular remedy for people who experience itchy eyes due to hay fever, dry eyes, or other allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago lanceolata tea is a natural remedy for itchy eyes. The plant is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. These help to soothe and calm the eyes, reducing redness and itching. The tea also contains a compound called mucilage, which has a protective and moisturizing effect on the eyes. When applied topically or consumed as a tea, mucilage helps to lock in moisture and calm inflammation, providing quick relief from itchy eyes. Additionally, Plantago lanceolata tea has antibacterial and antiviral properties, which can help to prevent infections that may be causing the itchiness. This natural remedy is gentle on the eyes and can be used as a treatment for various eye irritations, including dry eyes, conjunctivitis, and hay fever.

remedies.image_alt: Plantago lanceolata herbal teas for itchy eyes
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal tea for itchy eyes, start by gathering a handful of dried yarrow flowers (Achillea millefolium) or 2 teaspoons of fresh flowers. Rinse the flowers with cold water to remove any dirt. Next, heat 1 cup of water in a saucepan over low heat. Remove the water from the heat and add the dried yarrow flowers or fresh yarrow flowers to the water. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the water has turned a light yellow color and has a mild, slightly bitter taste. Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the yarrow flowers. Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature before consuming. To use the tea for itchy eyes, dip a clean cloth into the cooled tea and gently apply it to your closed eyes for 5-10 minutes. The anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties in yarrow may help to soothe and calm itchy eyes, providing relief from discomfort and irritation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium tea, also known as yarrow tea, is a natural remedy for itchy eyes. The leaves and flowers of the yarrow plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and redness in the eyes. When you drink yarrow tea, these compounds are absorbed into your body, where they can help to calm and soothe itchy eyes. Yarrow tea also contains antioxidants that can help to protect your eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to itchy eyes. Additionally, yarrow tea has been traditionally used to treat eye infections and allergies, which are common causes of itchy eyes. By drinking yarrow tea, you may be able to find relief from itchy eyes and promote overall eye health.

Luteolin is good for itchy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and calm the irritated tissues around the eyes, providing relief from itching and discomfort.

remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal teas for itchy eyes
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal tea for itchy eyes, start by gathering a handful of dried yarrow flowers or leaves, which are the parts of the plant that contain its medicinal properties. Rinse the dried herbs gently with cold water to remove any dirt or debris. Next, place one teaspoon of the dried yarrow herbs into a clean tea infuser or a small muslin bag. Heat one cup of water in a kettle or on the stovetop, and then pour the hot water over the yarrow herbs in the infuser or bag. Allow the herbs to steep for 5-7 minutes, which will help to release their active ingredients into the water. After the steeping time has passed, carefully remove the infuser or bag from the water and let it cool to room temperature. Once cooled, you can strain the liquid to remove any remaining herb particles and discard the solids. Finally, you can drink the yarrow tea up to 3 times a day as needed to help soothe itchy eyes. It is essential to note that while yarrow tea may provide relief from itchy eyes, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment if the symptoms persist or worsen.

supplementary_best: To remedy itchy eyes, a combination of Calendula officinalis, Euphrasia officinalis, and Aloe vera herbal teas may be effective. Calendula officinalis is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and soothe the eyes. Euphrasia officinalis, also known as eye herb, is traditionally used to treat eye irritations and conjunctivitis, and its antiseptic properties can help combat infection. Aloe vera is well-known for its soothing and moisturizing properties, which can help calm itchy eyes and reduce redness. This combination works well together because it addresses multiple aspects of itchy eyes - inflammation, infection, and dryness. By combining these three herbs, you can create a tea that provides a comprehensive approach to relieving itchy eyes. To make the tea, you can steep 1 teaspoon of each herb in boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink 1-2 cups per day as needed.
supplementary_avoid: If you have itchy eyes, it's best to avoid herbal teas that can irritate them further. One such herb is Ginkgo biloba. Its extracts can cause eye irritation and dryness due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which can be too strong for sensitive eyes. Another herb to avoid is Goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis). Its berberine content can cause eye inflammation and redness, making itchy eyes worse. Similarly, Eyebright (Glaucium flavum) might not be the best choice, as its alkaloids can cause eye irritation and dryness. Also, avoid teas made from Eyebright's relative, Ornithogalum umbellatum, as it too can cause eye problems. Additionally, teas made from Stinging Nettle (Urtica dioica) should be consumed with caution, as its urtic acid can cause skin irritation, which may spread to the eyes if not washed off properly. Finally, teas made from Wormwood (Artemisia absinthium) should be avoided, as its thujone content can cause eye irritation and dryness.
supplementary_other_remedies: If you have itchy eyes, there are some natural remedies you can try besides drinking herbal teas. One option is to use a warm compress. This is because the heat can help to loosen up any debris or allergens that might be causing the itchiness. To do this, you simply soak a cloth in warm water, wring it out, and then place it over your closed eyes for a few minutes. The heat can help to reduce inflammation and ease the itchiness. You can also try using a cool compress if the heat doesn't work for you - some people find that cool temperatures can be just as soothing.  Another natural remedy is to use a saline solution to rinse out your eyes. This is because the salt can help to flush out any irritants that might be causing the itchiness. You can buy saline solution at a pharmacy, or you can make your own by mixing salt and water. Simply put a few drops of the solution in your eyes and blink a few times to help spread it around.  You can also try using aloe vera gel to soothe itchy eyes. Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness and swelling. Simply apply a small amount of aloe vera gel to the affected area and let it sit for a few minutes before rinsing it off with water.  Some people find that applying a cold slice of cucumber to their eyes can help to reduce itchiness. The cooling effect of the cucumber can help to ease the itchiness and reduce inflammation. Simply chill a slice of cucumber in the fridge, then place it over your closed eyes for a few minutes.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with itchy eyes may also experience dry eyes, red eyes, and watery eyes due to inflammation or allergies. Medicinal herbs like aloe vera and chamomile can help soothe and calm the eyes. Aloe vera contains anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and redness, while chamomile has antihistamine properties that fight allergic reactions.  Those with itchy eyes may also be prone to conjunctivitis, an eye infection that causes pink eye. Echinacea and goldenseal are herbs that have antimicrobial properties, which can help fight off bacterial and viral infections that cause conjunctivitis.  Itchy eyes can also be a symptom of sinusitis, an infection of the sinuses. Eucalyptus and eplepsy are herbs that have decongestant properties, which can help clear out mucus and reduce swelling in the sinuses, relieving itchy eye symptoms.  People with itchy eyes may also experience eye strain and headaches due to prolonged use of computers or other digital devices. Ginkgo biloba and bilberry are herbs that improve blood flow to the eyes, reducing eye strain and promoting healing.  Note: Before using any herbal remedies, it's always best to consult with a healthcare professional to ensure safe use and to rule out any underlying conditions that may require medical attention.
